title: cupaniopsis shirleyana
intro: Cupaniopsis shirleyana, also known as the small-leaved native almond or Shirley's native almond, is a small tree native to Australia's eastern regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal effects. The health benefits of Cupaniopsis shirleyana include its potential to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into a paste or infusion, often used topically to apply directly to affected areas or taken internally as a tea or tincture.
